content: Hey, I'm so excited about my new YouTube channel. I just posted my first video about making croissants and it's getting a lot of views.
That sounds amazing, Shiloh! Congratulations on taking the leap and starting your channel. What inspired you to create content around baking?

Thanks! I've always loved baking, and as a Pastry Chef at Chez Pierre, I get to experiment with new recipes all the time. I'm thinking of doing a series on beekeeping next, since it's another one of my passions.
Beekeeping is fascinating! How did you get into it, and do you have any plans for incorporating it into your baking or YouTube channel?
I started beekeeping about 5 years ago, and I love how it helps me relax. I've been thinking of using my own honey in some of my recipes, maybe even doing a video on how to make honey-based desserts.
Using your own honey would definitely add a personal touch to your recipes. Have you considered collaborating with other chefs or beekeepers on your channel?

Actually, I've been talking to my colleague, Chef Emma, about doing a video together. She's an expert on artisanal cheeses, and we could do a pairing video with some of our favorite cheeses and honey-based desserts.
That sounds like a great idea! Cheese and honey pairings are always a hit. What's your favorite type of cheese to work with, and do you have a preferred honey variety?
I love working with truffle gouda, and my favorite honey is definitely the wildflower honey from my own beehives. I'm thinking of doing a video on how to make honey-infused truffle gouda next.
Wildflower honey is so unique and flavorful. Have you thought about selling your honey or honey-based products through your YouTube channel or at Chez Pierre?

That's a great idea! I've actually been thinking of starting a small online store, maybe by the end of the year. I'd love to sell my honey and some of my favorite pottery pieces, which I've been making in my free time.
An online store sounds like a fantastic venture. How do you plan on balancing your time between the restaurant, your YouTube channel, and your online store?

It'll definitely be a challenge, but I'm hoping to dedicate at least 10 hours a week to my channel and online store. I've also been trying to prioritize my triathlon training, so I can stay energized and focused.
That's impressive, Shiloh! You're really taking on a lot. How's your triathlon training going, and do you have any upcoming competitions?
It's going well, thanks for asking! I've got a competition coming up on June 15th, and I'm hoping to finish in under 2 hours. Wish me luck!
Good luck, Shiloh! We'll all be rooting for you. Don't forget to take care of yourself and prioritize your well-being, especially with all the exciting projects you have going on.
Thanks, I will! I just love staying busy and trying new things. Maybe I'll even do a video on how to make a honey-based energy drink for athletes next.
That's a great idea! I'm sure your audience would love that. Keep us posted on your progress, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need any help or advice along the way.
